title: Email Basics
drDigitalIntro: Email is how you send messages and documents over the internet. Unlike text messages, email is a more formal way to communicate — great for work, business, or longer conversations. Every email has a recipient (To), a subject line (what it's about), and a message body (your message). Email is free and can be used from any device. Mail is the app on your computer that handles email.

playgroundTask.question: What does the Subject line of an email tell the recipient?

drDigitalSuccess: Exactly — the Subject line is a preview of what your email is about. A clear subject helps the recipient know what to expect.
drDigitalHint: The Subject is like the title of a letter. It tells you what's inside before you open it.
